user_query:
I track watched eBay-style listings and want a simple event-triggered automation: when a new snapshot CSV of my watchlist lands in the workspace, run a command-line tool that filters, ranks, and produces a clear snipe plan for our next bidding session.

Use the provided sample snapshot at input/auctions_2026-04-18.csv and implement a CLI I can run like:
- bin/process_auctions input/auctions_2026-04-18.csv

Requirements:
1) Treat the CLI invocation as the “new file arrived” trigger. It must parse the CSV, apply the filters below, score and rank candidates, and write all outputs under output/.
2) Filters (a row is included only if all are true):
   - watchlist_flag == 1
   - time_left_min <= 180
   - seller_rating >= 97.0
   - (current_bid + shipping_cost) < my_max_bid
3) Scoring and ranking:
   - affordability_margin = my_max_bid - (current_bid + shipping_cost)
   - urgency_points = 3 if time_left_min <= 30; 2 if 30 < time_left_min <= 60; 1 if 60 < time_left_min <= 180; 0 otherwise
   - score = 0.5*affordability_margin + 10*(seller_rating/100) + 5*urgency_points - 0.5*bid_increment
   - Sort by score descending, then by time_left_min ascending, then by current_bid ascending. Select the top 5.
4) Outputs:
   - output/candidates.csv: a ranked table of the top 5 with columns (in this exact order): auction_id, title, current_bid, shipping_cost, time_left_min, seller_rating, bid_increment, my_max_bid, affordability_margin, urgency_points, score, rank. Values should reflect the computations above.
   - output/meeting_notes.md: meeting-ready notes with three sections:
     • Summary: include run timestamp (ISO format), input file name, total rows parsed, rows kept after filters, and a one-line recap of the ranking formula.
     • Top Targets: one line per candidate in rank order showing: rank, auction_id, title, score (rounded to 2 decimals), and snipe_offset_min_from_run = max(time_left_min - 1, 0).
     • Action Items: for each top target, include three bullets with (a) “Prepare snipe at recommended_bid = my_max_bid - bid_increment”, (b) “Verify seller feedback and item photos”, and (c) “Confirm total cost (current_bid + shipping_cost) is within budget”. Include the computed recommended_bid and total cost numbers inline for each item.
   - output/process_log.json: a small JSON object with fields: {"input_file": string, "processed_at": ISO timestamp, "total_rows": int, "filtered_in_rows": int, "top5_ids": array of auction_id strings in rank order, "outputs": {"candidates_csv": path, "meeting_notes_md": path}}
5) Determinism: Everything must be computed from the provided CSV; do not call external services or rely on live data. The CLI must be runnable locally and produce the three outputs at the exact paths above when given input/auctions_2026-04-18.csv.

This will help me quickly identify the best snipe targets and walk my team through action items during our daily bidding huddle. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
